title: T-022 — GRN from TO Server Action (transit→destination)
prompt:
# T-022 — GRN from TO Server Action

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §7.2, §7.5.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Server Action `receiveTransferOrder(toId, lpReceipts[])` moving transit LPs to destination location and updating to_lines.received_qty.

## Implementation contract
`apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/grn-from-to.ts`. Per receipt entry: locate LP at TRN-IN-{warehouse_code}, update LP.location_id and LP.warehouse_id to destination, insert stock_moves (move_type='receipt'). Update to_lines.received_qty. Trigger TO status transition per 04§7.4 (handled cross-module). Edge case: bulk-qty-no-LP transit creates new LP at receipt via T-016.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/grn-from-to.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/grn-from-to.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a transit LP with location_id=transit-in and shipped TO line, when service runs, then LP.location_id moves to destination location and warehouse_id updates.
2. Given a bulk-qty-no-LP TO, when service runs with operator-entered batch+qty, then a new LP is created at the destination location.
3. Given service completes, when to_lines is queried, then received_qty increments by the receipt quantity.

## Test strategy
- RED: grn-from-to.test.ts covers transit-LP receipt, bulk-no-LP creation, to_lines aggregation.
- Run `pnpm --filter web test grn-from-to`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not create duplicate LPs for the same transit shipment.
- Do not skip the receipt stock_move.
- Do not transition TO status here — emit handoff signal for 04-PLANNING.

pipeline_inputs.description: GRN-from-TO Server Action moving transit LPs to destination and updating to_lines aggregation.
pipeline_inputs.details: Existing LP path moves location/warehouse_id; bulk-no-LP path creates new LP at destination. Receipt stock_moves per LP. TO status transition delegated.

Given a bulk-qty-no-LP TO, when service runs with operator entries, then a new LP is created at destination
Given service completes, when to_lines is queried, then received_qty increments by the receipt quantity
